text: In software development, testing ensures that code works as expected. There are different types of tests. Unit tests check small parts of code, like individual functions, to make sure they are correct. Integration tests, on the other hand, verify that different parts of the system work together properly. Many developers use test-driven development, where they write tests before writing the actual code. This approach helps them think carefully about what the code should do. Overall, good testing gives developers confidence in their work. They can make changes or add new features without worrying about breaking existing functionality, which saves time and reduces stress.
